scenarios.description: String overload with non-null context and non-empty fragment returns nodes list (branch context!=null, loop-N)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(fakeTreeBuilder) where fakeTreeBuilder.parseFragment(reader,ctx,uri,parser) returns List.of(nodeA,nodeB); context Element ctx != null; no mocks beyond fakeTreeBuilder stub
pseudocode:
GIVEN parser using fakeTreeBuilder that returns two nodes; fragment="

A

B
"; context=Element.createShell("http://x"); baseUri="http://x"

scenarios.description: String overload with null context and empty fragment returns empty list (branch context==null, loop-0)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(fakeTreeBuilder) where fakeTreeBuilder.parseFragment(reader,null,uri,parser) returns emptyList; context=null
pseudocode: GIVEN parser with fakeTreeBuilder returning empty list; fragment=""; context=null; baseUri="http://x"

scenarios.description: Reader overload throws UncheckedIOException when Reader.read() IO error occurs (exception path)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(fakeTreeBuilder); Reader faultyReader throws IOException on read; no other mocks
pseudocode: GIVEN parser with default treeBuilder; fragmentReader throws IOException on any read; baseUri="http://x"

scenarios.description: Reader overload with non-null context returns single node list (branch context!=null, loop-1)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(fakeTreeBuilder) where fakeTreeBuilder.parseFragment returns List.of(nodeSingle); context Element exists
pseudocode: GIVEN parser using fakeTreeBuilder stub returning one node; fragmentReader=StringReader("X"); context=Element.createShell("u"); baseUri="u"

scenarios.description: String overload propagates UncheckedIOException from Reader overload (exception propagation path)
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(fakeTreeBuilder); fakeTreeBuilder.parseFragment not reached because StringReader(fragment) uses faultyReader throwing IOException; fragmentReader wraps IOException
pseudocode: GIVEN parser with default treeBuilder; fragmentReader=Reader that throws IOException on read; fragment string unused; baseUri="u"

scenarios.description: Reader overload with different baseUri and non-null context, returning multiple nodes to test varied baseUri handling
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(fakeTreeBuilder) where fakeTreeBuilder.parseFragment asserts passed baseUri and returns List.of(node1,node2,node3); context=Element.createShell("http://y")
pseudocode: GIVEN parser with fakeTreeBuilder checking baseUri=="http://y" and returning three nodes; fragmentReader=StringReader("<a/> <b/> <c/>"); context=Element.createShell("http://y"); baseUri="http://y"
